text:
{
  "page": 144,
  "confidence": "medium",
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "M-144-01",
      "region": "left margin",
      "type": "doodle",
      "description": "Small black silhouette of a head or helmet in profile with pointed top.",
      "scholarly_note": "Reproduced example of a marginal pen trial or doodle from f. 45r; profile heads are common informal marks in 13th-century Icelandic manuscripts."
    },
    {
      "id": "M-144-02",
      "region": "left margin",
      "type": "doodle",
      "description": "Elaborate line drawing of a creature with raised limbs or wings, pointed headdress, and surrounding curvilinear flourishes.",
      "scholarly_note": "Facsimile of a marginal grotesque or zoomorphic doodle from f. 45r; such figures served as space-fillers or scribal practice in Norse codices."
    },
    {
      "id": "M-144-03",
      "region": "left margin",
      "type": "doodle",
      "description": "Small profile drawing of a long-necked bird or animal with head turned back.",
      "scholarly_note": "Reproduced zoomorphic marginal mark; animal sketches frequently appear in blank areas of 13th-century Icelandic vellum."
    },
    {
      "id": "M-144-04",
      "region": "left margin",
      "type": "doodle",
      "description": "Heart- or shield-shaped form with finial beside a chalice-like vessel with three rising stems or fronds.",
      "scholarly_note": "Facsimile of symbolic or decorative marginalia from f. 45r; chalice and heart motifs may carry devotional or ornamental intent."
    },
    {
      "id": "M-144-05",
      "region": "text block",
      "type": "other",
      "description": "Multiple small cross (+) marks placed adjacent to letter forms in the upper and lower grids.",
      "scholarly_note": "Modern paleographic annotations added to flag variant or noteworthy letter shapes for study; not original to the manuscript."
    }
  ],
  "scratches": ["No surface scratches, abrasions, or scoring visible on either card."],
  "damage_notes": ["No holes, tears, creases, discoloration, or wear consistent with medieval vellum; the surfaces are modern printed cards in pristine condition."],
  "summary": "The image shows a modern two-card paleographic reference tool in a black plastic case displaying 13th-century letter forms labeled from f. 45r of Codex Regius (sec. XIII). Four small doodles occupy the left margin of the upper card reproducing marginal pen trials or decorative sketches (profile head, winged creature, bird, and chalice/heart). Numerous printed + marks appear within the text block grids as analytical annotations. Rulers, color terms (BRUN, ROSA, etc.), and labels are integral printed elements. The cards are clean with no stains, scratches, holes, offset ink, or scribal corrections visible; all non-grid marks are part of the modern reference design rather than features of an original manuscript page."
}

items.description: Small black silhouette of a head or helmet in profile with pointed top.
items.scholarly_note: Reproduced example of a marginal pen trial or doodle from f. 45r; profile heads are common informal marks in 13th-century Icelandic manuscripts.

items.description: Elaborate line drawing of a creature with raised limbs or wings, pointed headdress, and surrounding curvilinear flourishes.
items.scholarly_note: Facsimile of a marginal grotesque or zoomorphic doodle from f. 45r; such figures served as space-fillers or scribal practice in Norse codices.

items.description: Small profile drawing of a long-necked bird or animal with head turned back.
items.scholarly_note: Reproduced zoomorphic marginal mark; animal sketches frequently appear in blank areas of 13th-century Icelandic vellum.

items.description: Heart- or shield-shaped form with finial beside a chalice-like vessel with three rising stems or fronds.
items.scholarly_note: Facsimile of symbolic or decorative marginalia from f. 45r; chalice and heart motifs may carry devotional or ornamental intent.

items.description: Multiple small cross (+) marks placed adjacent to letter forms in the upper and lower grids.
items.scholarly_note: Modern paleographic annotations added to flag variant or noteworthy letter shapes for study; not original to the manuscript.

scratches: No surface scratches, abrasions, or scoring visible on either card.
damage_notes: No holes, tears, creases, discoloration, or wear consistent with medieval vellum; the surfaces are modern printed cards in pristine condition.
structuredOutput.summary: The image shows a modern two-card paleographic reference tool in a black plastic case displaying 13th-century letter forms labeled from f. 45r of Codex Regius (sec. XIII). Four small doodles occupy the left margin of the upper card reproducing marginal pen trials or decorative sketches (profile head, winged creature, bird, and chalice/heart). Numerous printed + marks appear within the text block grids as analytical annotations. Rulers, color terms (BRUN, ROSA, etc.), and labels are integral printed elements. The cards are clean with no stains, scratches, holes, offset ink, or scribal corrections visible; all non-grid marks are part of the modern reference design rather than features of an original manuscript page.
